summary: Fixed all critical and high-priority missing web endpoints to achieve parity with the Tauri desktop backend. Key changes: (1) Introduced a new WebEvent enum in state.rs that wraps both raw AgentEvent and the interactive approval/question/plan events — this allows the broadcast channel to carry all event types over WebSocket. (2) Updated state.rs to add pending_approval_reply, pending_question_reply, pending_plan_reply oneshot channels (matching the DesktopBridge pattern), plus last_session_id and edit_history for retry/undo support. (3) Updated submit_goal in api.rs to spawn three additional forwarder tasks (approval, question, plan) that drain their respective receivers during each agent run, populate the pending reply channels, and broadcast WebEvent variants over the WS channel. (4) Added 14 new HTTP handlers covering: resolve_approval, resolve_question, resolve_plan (interactive flow completion); retry_last_message, edit_and_resend, regenerate_response, undo_last_edit (message actions); get_current_model, switch_model (model management); get_config (settings panel hydration); get_permission_level, set_permission_level, toggle_permission_level (permission management); search_sessions (session search). (5) Added QuestionRequest and PlanCreated variants to WebAgentEvent, and updated convert_agent_event to convert_web_event which handles all WebEvent variants. (6) Fixed clear_message_queue to actually cancel the agent (draining the steering queue) for 'all'/'steering' targets instead of being a no-op. (7) Registered all new routes in mod.rs. cargo check --workspace and cargo test -p ava-tui both pass cleanly (0 errors, 0 warnings, 12/12 tests pass).
